phases.name: Phase 0
phases.label: Full Runway OS Installation And Baseline

phases.name: Phase 5
phases.label: UX Polish, Operations, And Durable Handoff

phaseWorkBlocks.label: 0A
phaseWorkBlocks.name: Full Runway OS Install
phaseWorkBlocks.whyThisAgent: The block requires scaffold installation, source-of-truth judgment, cross-file integration, local verification, and dashboard stewardship.
stopConditions: An existing project file would be overwritten
Verification requires credentials, production data, or a live financial mutation
The scope expands into application implementation or production operations
Meaningful Runway OS health checks cannot be adapted to the existing repo shape
verification: node command-center/scripts/refresh-dashboard.js
node command-center/scripts/health-check.js
.venv/bin/python scripts/smoke_test.py
git diff --check
generated dashboard inspection
git status --short --branch
phaseWorkBlocks.reportBack: Reported created surfaces, passing verification, preserved files, local-only durability, proposed roadmap, and the first operational block for Ryan review.
phaseWorkBlocks.result: Installed and verified the full Runway OS command center without changing application or production behavior.

phaseWorkBlocks.label: 1A
phaseWorkBlocks.name: Restore Daily Sync And Operational Baseline
phaseWorkBlocks.whyThisAgent: The block requires a controlled GitHub mutation, sanitized workflow intake, live-system stop judgment, and Runway OS closeout.
phaseWorkBlocks.expectedLiveEffect: Enable the scheduled workflow and dispatch one run that may insert newly available transactions and invoke the existing Luxe Legacy bridge.
stopConditions: GitHub authorization or workflow access is unavailable
The workflow targets an unexpected endpoint
The run exposes credential or financial-row output
Any entity sync, downstream bridge, production check, or workflow job fails
Recovery requires source-code, secret, Fly, database, or authentication changes
verification: Record workflow state before and after enable
Confirm one manual dispatch and wait for completion
Record only sanitized per-entity success or failure
Confirm the workflow remains active
Verify production and demo root HTTP status
Refresh and health-check Runway OS
phaseWorkBlocks.reportBack: Reported exact workflow state change, sanitized controlled-run outcome, HTTP health baseline, stop conditions, Runway OS closeout, and target durability.
phaseWorkBlocks.result: Changed the workflow from disabled_inactivity to active; controlled run 29627530457 and all steps passed; production and demo roots returned HTTP 200.

phaseWorkBlocks.label: 0B
phaseWorkBlocks.name: Target Durability Closeout
phaseWorkBlocks.whyThisAgent: The block requires exact staging boundaries, source-of-truth verification, and non-force target-branch push.
stopConditions: Staging includes any path outside PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md and command-center
Verification regresses
Push requires force or encounters unexpected remote divergence
verification: Inspect staged paths and diff summary
Commit the verified baseline
Push codex/runway-os-full-install without force
Verify upstream branch and remote commit
phaseWorkBlocks.reportBack: Transitioned to active Phase 1 work block 1A after the verified baseline was committed and pushed.
phaseWorkBlocks.result: Committed and pushed the verified Runway OS baseline as e9a8e5e on origin/codex/runway-os-full-install.

phaseWorkBlocks.label: 1B
phaseWorkBlocks.name: Define Recurring Sync Safeguards
phaseWorkBlocks.whyThisAgent: The block is sanitized platform research, option comparison, recommendation, and Runway OS planning work.
stopConditions: Recommendation requires credential, financial, production, or row-level data
A new external service, recurring cost, or write-capable automation would be selected without Ryan direction
Platform behavior cannot be verified authoritatively
verification: Cite authoritative platform behavior in the project log
Compare at least two viable safeguards
Recommend one implementation-ready path
Refresh and health-check Runway OS without live mutation
phaseWorkBlocks.reportBack: Reported the verified platform behavior, five option tradeoffs, independent alert-only monitor recommendation, and proposed work block 1C.
phaseWorkBlocks.result: Matched disabled_inactivity to GitHub's documented public-repo 60-day rule, recommended an independent read-only Codex monitor, and separated top-of-hour cron hardening into Task 6.

phaseWorkBlocks.label: 1C
phaseWorkBlocks.name: Independent Daily Sync Monitor
excludedTasks: Workflow edits or automatic enable, dispatch, or rerun
Plaid, Fly, application, secret, database, or financial-data access
Paid external services, commit, push, PR, merge, or deploy
phaseWorkBlocks.whyThisAgent: The block requires project-local automation setup, exact sensitive-action boundaries, read-only validation, and Runway OS closeout.
phaseWorkBlocks.expectedLiveEffect: Create one local recurring Codex automation that reads unauthenticated public GitHub metadata daily at 7:00 AM America/Chicago and may notify Ryan without mutating the workflow or financial system.
alertConditions: No schedule event exists within the preceding 36 hours
Latest scheduled run completed with a conclusion other than success
Latest scheduled run remains incomplete beyond the conservative delay window
stopConditions: The automation requires credentials, private data, or a paid external service
It cannot distinguish scheduled runs from manual dispatches
Healthy checks would create unavoidable noisy daily alerts
It would enable, dispatch, rerun, or edit the workflow
A read-only test cannot verify current workflow state and scheduled-run freshness
verification: Inspect the automation definition before activation
Run one read-only test against current public workflow metadata
Confirm active state and identify the latest scheduled run without opening logs
Verify no workflow, application, or production state changed
Refresh and health-check Runway OS
phaseWorkBlocks.reportBack: Reported automation identity, cadence, alert thresholds, read-only test result, limitations, and the separate Task 6 boundary.
phaseWorkBlocks.result: Created active automation expense-tracker-daily-plaid-sync-monitor; its unauthenticated public-metadata test identified active workflow 256886458 and successful scheduled run 29640666471 with no alert conditions, while excluding manual dispatch 29627530457.

phaseWorkBlocks.label: 1D
phaseWorkBlocks.name: Harden Daily Sync Schedule
excludedTasks: Workflow enable, disable, dispatch, or rerun and manual Plaid sync
Application, Fly configuration, secret, credential, financial-data, database, authentication, monitor, or parent-repo changes
phaseWorkBlocks.whyThisAgent: The block is a small sensitive-repo edit coupled to GitHub release handling, production-deploy observation, and Runway OS closeout.
phaseWorkBlocks.expectedLiveEffect: Merge one ready PR to main, triggering one production Fly deploy; do not manually dispatch Plaid sync.
stopConditions: The diff expands beyond confirmed workflow and command-center paths
Remote divergence, branch protection, or PR state changes the release plan
Local verification, Fly Deploy, production HTTP health, dashboard refresh, or health check fails
Verification requires secrets, financial data, response-body logs, or manual Plaid execution
The skip-annotated closeout push unexpectedly starts another Fly deploy
Recovery requires a new production or financial-system mutation
verification: Confirm only the cron and explanatory comment change workflow behavior
Run the synthetic smoke suite and git diff --check
Refresh and health-check Runway OS before release
Merge the ready PR and verify default-branch cron source
Confirm Daily Plaid Sync remains active and the independent monitor is unchanged
Confirm Fly Deploy succeeds and production plus demo roots return HTTP 200
Close Phase 1 and publish the closeout with skip actions without a second deploy
phaseWorkBlocks.reportBack: Reported exact cron change, branch, PR, commit and deploy evidence, HTTP health, workflow state, protected boundaries, dashboard verification, and pending natural-run observation.
phaseWorkBlocks.result: Source commit e34c239 changed the cron to minute 17 and merged through ready PR 83 as 96af7dc. Fly Deploy run 29645346441 and every step passed; the workflow remained active and production plus demo returned HTTP 200 without a manual sync or sensitive log access.

phaseWorkBlocks.label: 2A
phaseWorkBlocks.name: Restore the Root Project Entry Point
excludedTasks: Application code, workflows, databases, production, Plaid, Fly, credentials, financial data, merge, or deployment
PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE.md, plan.md, CLAUDE.md, untracked AGENTS.md, untracked scripts/sync_prod_to_local.sh, and parent-repo changes
phaseWorkBlocks.whyThisAgent: The block requires source-of-truth reconciliation, sensitive-project boundaries, local verification, command-center stewardship, and exact-scope GitHub durability.
expectedFiles: PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md if its README classification needs updating
stopConditions: A claim cannot be verified without credentials, ignored files, or row-level financial data
Implementation requires application code, workflow, legacy-document, or pre-existing untracked-file changes
A Ryan decision about archiving legacy documents or tracking AGENTS.md becomes necessary
Scope expands beyond Tasks 1 and 4
Smoke, documentation, dashboard, health, or exact-scope verification fails in a plan-changing way
Branch or draft-PR publication would include paths outside the confirmed scope
verification: Cross-check README claims against runtime, Flask factory, Fly configurations, workflows, .env.example, requirements, and current repository structure
Confirm stale Streamlit, no-bank-linking, and two-entity instructions are removed
Validate referenced paths and commands
Run .venv/bin/python scripts/smoke_test.py
Run git diff --check
Refresh and health-check Runway OS
Inspect the generated dashboard and exact staged paths
Confirm the pushed branch and draft PR contain no application, ignored-data, legacy-document, or untracked-file changes
phaseWorkBlocks.reportBack: Returned the new README structure, important truth corrections, passing verification, pushed branch, source commit, draft PR, preserved boundaries, and isolated governance uncertainty.
phaseWorkBlocks.durability: Source commit c249c9b is pushed on origin/codex/phase-2-root-docs and included in draft PR 84; the verified closeout is on the same branch, with no merge or deployment.
phaseWorkBlocks.result: Replaced the retired Streamlit, manual-import-only, two-entity README with a tracked-source-verified Flask, HTMX, Plaid, Fly.io, and three-entity entry point; added sanitized operating boundaries; updated PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md; preserved every excluded file; passed synthetic, documentation, exact-scope, dashboard, and health verification; and opened draft PR 84 without merge or deployment.

phaseWorkBlocks.label: 2A-R
phaseWorkBlocks.name: Publish the Root Project Entry Point
excludedTasks: Content changes beyond the verified PR 84 diff
Application code, workflows, monitor changes, databases, Plaid, credentials, financial data, Fly configuration or secrets, legacy or untracked files, parent-repo changes, and out-of-plan recovery
phaseWorkBlocks.whyThisAgent: The release requires exact-scope GitHub mutation, production-deploy observation, stop judgment, and Runway OS closeout.
phaseWorkBlocks.expectedLiveEffect: Mark PR 84 ready and merge it to main, triggering one automatic production Fly deploy; then push a command-center-only closeout with skip actions so no second deploy starts.
stopConditions: PR 84 is no longer open, clean, or limited to the verified eight paths
Main diverges or branch protection changes the release path
Synthetic smoke, dashboard, health, or exact-diff verification regresses
Fly Deploy fails or production root or health does not return HTTP 200
Verification requires logs, secrets, financial data, Plaid, databases, or manual workflow dispatch
Recovery requires application, authentication, infrastructure, credential, or financial-system mutation
The closeout push would unexpectedly trigger a second Fly deploy
verification: Recheck exact PR files, open draft state, and clean mergeability
Rerun the synthetic smoke suite, Runway OS refresh and health, and git diff --check
Mark PR 84 ready and merge without force
Identify the resulting main merge commit and Fly Deploy run
Inspect only sanitized workflow, job, and step status without logs
Confirm production health and root return HTTP 200
Return Task 2 to current, close 2A-R, refresh and health-check Runway OS, and push the closeout with skip actions
phaseWorkBlocks.reportBack: Return the merge commit, deploy run and result, production HTTP health, final main and origin/main state, protected boundaries, closeout commit, and remaining Phase 2 decision.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Inventory the existing repo and preserve pre-existing state
phaseTasks.summary: Record repo identity, planning surfaces, safe checks, live workflow state, and closed data boundaries.
phaseTasks.result: Read-only intake completed; tracked baseline was clean and two pre-existing untracked files were preserved.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Install the full Runway OS scaffold on a dedicated branch
phaseTasks.summary: Stage the standard scaffold separately and transplant only new Runway OS surfaces.
phaseTasks.result: Created codex/runway-os-full-install and added PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md plus the complete command-center tree without overwriting existing files.

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Migrate current project-control truth
phaseTasks.summary: Replace generic scaffold placeholders with Expense Tracker's canonical current state, roadmap, decisions, rules, architecture, issues, and migration map.
phaseTasks.result: Created canonical project-control sources, the source migration map, parked issues, and proposed multiphase roadmap.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Adapt the verification contract to the existing repo shape
phaseTasks.summary: Require existing product roots for retrofit profiles instead of scratch-only app and scratch folders.
phaseTasks.result: Health checks now preserve and validate the existing web, core, and scripts product roots.

phaseTasks.title: Task 5: Verify and close the bootstrap
phaseTasks.summary: Refresh and inspect the dashboard, run Runway OS health checks, rerun the synthetic smoke suite, and verify the diff and worktree.
phaseTasks.result: Refresh, health check, synthetic smoke suite, JSON validation, visual inspection, and whitespace checks passed.

phaseTasks.title: Task 6: Review the proposed roadmap and authorize the first operational block
phaseTasks.summary: Confirm or change the proposed phases and approve the exact Phase 1 work block before any external or production mutation.
phaseTasks.result: Ryan accepted Phases 1-5, target-branch durability, and work block 1A with its documented live effects.

phaseTasks.title: Task 7: Commit and push the verified Runway OS baseline
phaseTasks.summary: Stage only the approved Runway OS paths, commit the install branch, and push it without force.
phaseTasks.result: Staged only approved Runway OS paths, committed e9a8e5e, and pushed the branch without force.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Re-enable the disabled Daily Plaid Sync workflow with Ryan authorization
phaseTasks.summary: Restore the intended schedule through an explicitly approved external GitHub mutation.
phaseTasks.result: Changed workflow ID 256886458 from disabled_inactivity to active.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Verify one controlled sync execution
phaseTasks.summary: Verify all-entity results and failure reporting without exposing financial row data.
phaseTasks.result: Controlled workflow-dispatch run 29627530457 completed successfully; the sync job and all job steps passed without opening response-body logs.

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Establish production and demo health baselines
phaseTasks.summary: Capture safe HTTP, deploy, and workflow health checks in Runway OS.
phaseTasks.result: Daily Plaid Sync and Fly Deploy are active; production and demo roots returned HTTP 200.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Define recurring operational checks
phaseTasks.summary: Decide what remains manual and what later becomes a monitored automation.
phaseTasks.result: Compared five safeguards and recommended an independent alert-only Codex monitor; recorded top-of-hour cron hardening separately.

phaseTasks.title: Task 5: Add an independent read-only Daily Plaid Sync monitor
phaseTasks.summary: Create a project-local recurring Codex automation that alerts on disabled state, missing scheduled runs, or a non-successful latest scheduled run without workflow writes.
phaseTasks.result: Created and activated expense-tracker-daily-plaid-sync-monitor on a daily 7:00 AM local schedule; read-only scheduled-event validation passed with no alert conditions.

phaseTasks.title: Task 6: Move the daily trigger away from the start of the hour
phaseTasks.summary: Reduce GitHub's documented top-of-hour delay/drop exposure through a separate source-and-release block.
phaseTasks.result: Changed the default-branch schedule to 17 9 * * * through PR 83; Fly Deploy run 29645346441 passed, Daily Plaid Sync remained active, and production plus demo returned HTTP 200.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Rebuild the root README for the current architecture
phaseTasks.summary: Replace retired Streamlit and no-bank-linking guidance with the current Flask, Plaid, Fly.io, and three-entity system.
phaseTasks.nextAction: Publish the verified PR 84 documentation to main and verify the resulting production deploy in work block 2A-R.
phaseTasks.blocker: None inside the confirmed release scope; stop on PR drift, deploy failure, non-200 health, or any need for manual recovery.
phaseTasks.result: README.md now provides a current tracked-source-verified project entry point on draft PR 84.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Decide the future of PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE.md and plan.md
phaseTasks.summary: Approve archive, replacement, or historical labels after useful content is migrated.
phaseTasks.nextAction: Review draft PR 84 and a separately proposed governance block before changing preserved legacy files.
phaseTasks.blocker: Awaiting Ryan review and work-block confirmation

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Reconcile CLAUDE.md and the untracked AGENTS.md
phaseTasks.summary: Define one maintained instruction source and decide whether AGENTS.md becomes tracked.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Document deployment, data, and side-effect boundaries
phaseTasks.summary: Create current operator guidance without credentials or row-level financial detail.
phaseTasks.result: README.md now documents configuration names, deployment mechanics, data protection, and explicit authorization gates without secret values or financial detail.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Audit core workflows for all three entities
phaseTasks.summary: Evaluate Personal, BFM, and Luxe Legacy behavior under explicit data-handling rules.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Audit product subsystems
phaseTasks.summary: Review sync, import, matching, categorization, reporting, planning, payroll, weekly, waterfall, PWA, and public dashboard behavior.

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Convert findings into ranked issues
phaseTasks.summary: Record reproduction, impact, severity, and acceptance checks.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Confirm repair order and work blocks
phaseTasks.summary: Approve the implementation sequence before fixes begin.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Repair confirmed reliability and correctness defects
phaseTasks.summary: Implement prioritized fixes in bounded verified work blocks.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Expand regression coverage
phaseTasks.summary: Add synthetic tests around repaired workflows and entity isolation.

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Add safe CI checks
phaseTasks.summary: Create private-repo CI that uses only synthetic data and no live credentials.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Deploy an explicitly approved release
phaseTasks.summary: Deploy only after local and CI verification plus a release approval gate.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Review desktop, mobile, and installed-PWA workflows
phaseTasks.summary: Evaluate usability and accessibility after core repairs.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Polish high-friction journeys
phaseTasks.summary: Improve confirmed friction without widening financial or authentication risk.

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Finalize runbooks and release evidence
phaseTasks.summary: Make project re-entry, monitoring, and maintenance durable.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Complete target and parent durability closeout
phaseTasks.summary: Commit and push target history and record a compact sanitized parent pointer when authorized.

artifacts.name: Work Block 2A Root Project Entry Point Closeout

recentActivity.text: Completed read-only existing-project intake and identified a disabled Daily Plaid Sync workflow.
Ryan confirmed a full in-repo Runway OS install rather than a thin overlay.
Created codex/runway-os-full-install and added the standard protected-retrofit scaffold without overwriting existing files.
Completed work block 0A with passing Runway OS health, synthetic application smoke, visual dashboard inspection, and whitespace checks.
Ryan accepted Phases 1-5, authorized target-branch commit/push, and confirmed work block 1A with its controlled live effects.
Committed and pushed the verified full Runway OS baseline as e9a8e5e on origin/codex/runway-os-full-install.
Completed work block 1A: Daily Plaid Sync is active, controlled run 29627530457 passed, and production/demo returned HTTP 200.
Recorded work block 1B as confirmed, reconciled the already-completed merge to main, and completed sanitized safeguard research.
Completed work block 1B: matched recurrence to GitHub's 60-day public-repo inactivity rule, recommended an independent alert-only monitor, and proposed 1C.
Ryan authorized committing and pushing the completed 1B command-center closeout directly to main while preserving pre-existing untracked files.
Ryan confirmed work block 1C for a daily project-local read-only monitor with failure-only alerts and no workflow or financial-system mutation.
Completed work block 1C: active automation expense-tracker-daily-plaid-sync-monitor passed its public scheduled-run metadata test without workflow or financial-system mutation.
Committed and pushed the completed 1C command-center closeout as b1742cf directly to main while preserving pre-existing untracked files; the dashboard durability record followed on main.
Ryan confirmed work block 1D for the minute-17 cron edit, ready PR and merge, one resulting production Fly deploy, and a skip-annotated sanitized closeout.
Completed work block 1D: PR 83 merged the minute-17 schedule as 96af7dc; Fly Deploy run 29645346441 passed; Daily Plaid Sync stayed active; production and demo returned HTTP 200.
Closed Phase 1 and activated Phase 2 for just-in-time work-block planning without authorizing documentation implementation.
Ryan confirmed work block 2A for Tasks 1 and 4: restore the root README and sanitized operating boundaries on codex/phase-2-root-docs, then publish a draft PR without merge or deployment.
Completed work block 2A: rebuilt the root project entry point, passed synthetic and Runway OS verification, pushed source commit c249c9b, and opened draft PR 84 without merge or deployment.
Ryan authorized release block 2A-R to merge verified PR 84 to main, observe one automatic production Fly deploy, and publish a skip-actions closeout.

Which legacy documentation should be replaced, archived, or retained after migration?
